content: The type of fit between a mating shaft of diameter $25.0^{\begin{matrix} +0.010 \\\ -0.010 \end{matrix}}$ mm and a hole of diameter $25.0^{\begin{matrix} +0.015 \\\ -0.015 \end{matrix}}$ is ______.
explanation:
The dimensions of the mating shaft and hole are as follows:

  • Shaft: $25.0^{+0.010}_{-0.010} \, \text{mm}$

  • Hole: $25.0^{+0.015}_{-0.015} \, \text{mm}$

Calculations for limits:

For the shaft:

  • Upper limit: $25.01 \, \text{mm}$

  • Lower limit: $24.99 \, \text{mm}$

For the hole:

  • Upper limit: $25.03 \, \text{mm}$

  • Lower limit: $25.00 \, \text{mm}$

This configuration results in a transition fit.

Therefore, the correct option is (B).

content: Bolts in the flanged end of pressure vessel are usually pre- tensioned. Indicate which of the following statements is not true

options.content: Pre- tensioning helps to seal the pressure vessel
Pre- tensioning increase the fatigue life of the bolts
Pre- tensioning reduces the maximum tensile stress in the bolts
Pre- tensioning helps to reduce the effect of pressure pulsations in the pressure vessels

options.content: none of these because in fabricated beams welds do not get stressed.

content:
Two plates of thickness 10 mm each are to be joined by a transverse fillet weld on one side and the resulting structure is loaded as shown in the figure below.

If the ultimate tensile strength of the weld material is 150 MPa and the factor of safety to be used is 3 , the minimum length of the weld required to ensure that the weld does NOT fail is __________ mm (rounded off to 2 decimal places).
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} S_{y s}=150 \mathrm{MPa}, N=3, & l=? \\ P & =0.707 \times t \times l_e \times \frac{S_{y s}}{N} \\ 5000 & =0.707 \times 10 \times l \times \frac{150}{3} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { }\\ &\begin{aligned}Now, if \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, l & =14.14 \mathrm{~mm} \\ S_{y t} & =150 \mathrm{MPa} \quad \text { (as given in some memory based questions) } \\ P & =0.707 t l_e \tau_{\text {per }} \\ P & =0.707 t l \times \frac{S_{y t}}{2 N}\left(\therefore S_{y s}=\frac{S_{y t}}{2} \text { and } l_e=l\right) \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} 5000 & =0.707 \times 10 \times l \times \frac{150}{2 \times 3} \\ \therefore \quad l & =28.28 \mathrm{~mm} \\ \text { Ans: } \quad 14.14 \mathrm{~mm} \text { if } S_{y s} & =150 \mathrm{MPa} \\ 28.28 \mathrm{~mm} \text { if } S_{y t} & =150 \mathrm{MPa} \end{aligned} $$

content: A bracket is attached to a vertical column by means of two identical rivets U and V separated by a distance of 2a = 100 mm, as shown in the figure. The permissible shear stress of the rivet material is 50 MPa. If a load P = 10 kN is applied at an eccentricity e = 3√7 a, the minimum cross-sectional area of each of the rivets to avoid failure is ___________ mm2.
explanation:
Given :

Load, $ P = 10 \text{kN} = 10,000 \text{N} $

Distance, $ 2a = 100 \text{mm} $

Eccentricity, $ e = 3\sqrt{7}a $

Primary force :

$ P_p = \frac{10,000}{2} = 5,000 \text{N} $

Secondary force :

Using the formula for the secondary force :

$ P \cdot e = \frac{P \cdot S_1}{2a} \cdot 4a^2 $

Solving for $ S_1 $ :

$ 10 \times 10^3 \times 3\sqrt{7}a = P \cdot S_1 \cdot 2a $

$ P \cdot S_1 = 39,686.26 \text{N} $

Resultant force $ R $ :

$ R = \sqrt{P_p^2 + P_s^2} $

$ R = \sqrt{5000^2 + 39686.26^2} $

$ R_{\max} = 40,000 \text{N} $

Calculating the area $ A $ :

$ \frac{R_{\max}}{A} = \tau $

$ \frac{40,000}{50} = A $

$ A = 800 \text{mm}^2 $
